fix(db): Change OSCONJ column type on form_eye_antseg table (#11502)

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Your PR title must follow Conventional Commits: type(scope): description
Examples: feat(api): add patient search, fix(calendar): correct date
parsing
See CONTRIBUTING.md for details

Please provide context about the nature of your change so that reviewers
understand the motivation and we can generate changelogs.

If this resolves an existing issue, link to one or more issues in the
short description section, e.g. `Fixes #12345`.

-->

#### Short description of what this changes or resolves:
OSCONJ column type on form_ant_antseg table = varchar(25) while column
type = text for ODCONJ and other related columns on table.
This was a problem because users would enter >25 characters in the
OSCONJ field on Anterior Segment section (form_eye_antseg) of the Eye
Form, but only the first 25 characters would save. Related fields in the
section (ODCONJ) allowed and saved entry of >25 characters.

#### Changes proposed in this pull request:
Change OSCONJ column type to text so that it behaves same as related
fields in eye form section.

#### Test Plan
Ran sql upgrade from 8.1.0 after making changes in
8_1_0-to-8_1_1_upgrade.sql without incident and form/field behaved as
expected.

No

commit.message:
Fix missing KO moves: check HP before applying hpState in filter

The post-processing filter was applying hpState BEFORE checking if the
target was already fainted. Since hpState for a KO move shows the target
at 0 HP, the filter incorrectly treated the KO move itself as hitting
an already-dead target and removed it.

Fix: check target HP using tracked state from PREVIOUS entries, then
apply the current entry's hpState for subsequent checks.

Result: 100% move retention (1338/1338 raw moves in parsed log).

commit.message:
[jaeger_mcp] replace logging of MCP methods with tracing (#8160)

## Which problem is this PR solving?
- Replaces MCP method logging with tracing in `jaeger_mcp`.
- keep HTTP transport observability in `otelhttp`, and capture MCP/GenAI
semantics in MCP middleware spans.

## Description of the changes
- Removed MCP logging middleware:
- deleted
`cmd/jaeger/internal/extension/jaegermcp/logging_middleware.go`
- Added tracing middleware for MCP methods:
  - file: `cmd/jaeger/internal/extension/jaegermcp/middleware.go`
  - spans are created for all MCP methods
- Tool-call enrichment (`tools/call` only):
  - span name includes tool name (`tools/call <tool>`)
  - attributes:
    - `gen_ai.operation.name=execute_tool`
    - `gen_ai.tool.name=<tool>`
- Non-tool MCP methods:
  - include `mcp.method.name`
- Session context:
  - include `mcp.session.id` when present
- Error behavior:
- transport/handler errors (`err != nil`): `RecordError` + span status
`ERROR`
- tool business errors (`CallToolResult.IsError`): set
`error.type=tool_error`, record concrete tool error when provided, do
not force span status `ERROR`
- Switched MCP/GenAI semconv usage to Jaeger’s internal wrapper:
  - `internal/telemetry/otelsemconv`
- Simplified server tool registration:
  - removed thin `addTool(...)` wrapper
  - use direct `mcp.AddTool(...)` calls in `server.go`

## How was this change tested?
- `make fmt`
- `make lint`
- `make test`
- `go test ./cmd/jaeger/internal/extension/jaegermcp/...`

- Manual verification in Jaeger UI with real MCP calls:
  - `initialize`
  - `tools/call` -> `get_services`
  - `tools/call` -> `get_span_names`
  - `tools/call` -> `search_traces`
  - failure path: invalid tool-call input / invalid trace id

## Checklist
- [x] I have read
https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING_GUIDELINES.md
- [x] I have signed all commits
- [x] I have added unit tests for the new functionality
- [x] I have run lint and test steps successfully: `make lint test`

- [ ] **None**

- [ ] **Heavy**

fix(openclaw): remove gateway restart that broke TUI pairing

causing the gateway to re-initialize and invalidate the paired.json
scope patch, leaving new containers with 'pairing required' in
openclaw TUI. The gateway auto-watches its config file for changes,
so the explicit restart was never needed.

commit.message:
fix(bench): disable Maglev execution to prevent SIGSEGV in 4 benchmarks

Maglev codegen always deopts at the start due to CreateMappedArguments
falling to the catch-all handler. For simple benchmarks (fib, arithmetic,
property_access), the deopt works correctly. For complex programs
(closures, constructors, deep chains, keyed stores), the deopt triggers
SIGSEGV. Since the result is always a deopt back to the interpreter,
disabling execution has zero performance impact.

This unblocks all 9 precompiled benchmarks so we get complete numbers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use 256MB demand-paged pool for l_img.h

Replaces the 64MB fixed pool with 256MB virtual reservation.
Pages are demand-paged (MAP_ANONYMOUS) — only physical memory for
pages actually touched is committed. Handles images up to ~8000x8000.

The grow-on-demand approach was reverted because stb_image holds
raw pointers into the pool that can't be relocated.
